question.label: Friction scan
question.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
question.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

answer: The last stretch shipped, but it was heavier than I made it sound.
note: Says the work shipped but was heavier than presented, which signals hidden strain and some mismatch between apparent and actual load.

question.label: Heavier than sounded
question.name: When you say it was 'heavier' — what was adding the weight?
question.description: Drills into the specific source of the hidden load Lin just named.

answer: Late nights came from carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews.
note: Carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews signals strong overload and ownership concentration dragging engagement; also points to a clarity/delegation issue worth probing next.

question.label: Decision bottlenecks
question.name: Those decision bottlenecks — where are they still sticking on you?
question.description: Drills into the specific decisions Lin is still holding so the load source becomes actionable.

answer: Some of that work could have moved to the team sooner.
note: Lin says work that stayed with them could have been handed to the team sooner, which signals mild drag from over-holding ownership rather than active momentum.

question.label: Opening pace check
question.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
question.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

answer: I am proud of the outcomes, but the pace should not be normal.
note: Proud of the outcomes signals some investment, but saying the pace should not be normal is a strong unsustainable-pace wellbeing signal.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: proud outcomes, pace — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: I still do not get enough substantive feedback on key decisions.
note: Names a lack of substantive feedback on key decisions, which signals unsupported strain and mild disengagement; also reveals a clarity/support issue worth probing next.

question.label: Key decisions feedback
question.name: On those key decisions, what feedback are you not getting that would help most?
question.description: Drills into the missing feedback Lin named so the manager can understand the specific support gap.

answer: I want clearer ownership boundaries so I am not default escalation.
note: Names a missing condition — clear ownership boundaries so Lin is not the default escalation — which is a strong growth-support gap and mild drag on engagement.

question.label: Priority clarity
question.name: What are you actually focused on this week, and why those things?
question.description: Surfaces whether priorities are crisp or vague; whether they match what you thought.

answer: Next I want to hand off one stream end-to-end and stay out of the middle.
note: Names a specific near-term priority and a concrete shift in role — handing off one stream end-to-end to reduce middle-layer involvement.

question.label: Ownership boundaries
question.name: Where are ownership boundaries still fuzzy this week?
question.description: Pinpoints the specific work areas where Lin is still becoming the default escalation.

answer: If we reset scope now, the next month is sustainable.
note: Names a specific condition — resetting scope now — that would make the next month sustainable, which is a mild positive clarity signal.

question.label: Next two weeks steadier
question.name: What would make the next two weeks feel steadier for you?
question.description: Ends by converting the conversation into a concrete ask or adjustment without opening a new thread.

answer: I want to focus first on unblocking the one dependency that is slowing the team the most this week.
note: Names a concrete near-term focus and priority, which signals some steadiness through clearer immediate direction.
